evals.prompt: I need to update my confluent-kafka-python from 2.13.0 to the latest version. My project is a simple sensor data producer using the sync Producer API with Avro serialization. Can you upgrade it?
evals.expected_output: Updates requirements.txt version pin to latest (2.14.0), reviews producer.py for breaking changes, reports what changed

assertions.name: extras_preserved
assertions.description: The [avro] extras are preserved in the dependency pin

assertions.name: producer_code_intact
assertions.description: Producer import and usage are still present and correct

assertions.name: explained_changes
assertions.description: The output explains what changed between versions

evals.prompt: We're on confluent-kafka 2.11.0 and need to upgrade to 2.14.0. Our order processing app has a producer and consumer. I'm worried about breaking changes — can you handle the upgrade and fix anything that breaks?
evals.expected_output: Updates version pin, identifies config values passed as int/bool that will break with v2.13.0 type hint enforcement, fixes them, checks consumer for issues

assertions.name: producer_config_types_fixed
assertions.description: Producer config no longer has bare int values for message.max.bytes — should be quoted strings or otherwise corrected

assertions.name: consumer_config_reviewed
assertions.description: Output mentions reviewing or fixing the consumer config types

assertions.name: breaking_changes_explained
assertions.description: Output explains the type hinting enforcement in 2.13.0

evals.prompt: I have a FastAPI app that uses confluent-kafka-python for producing events. We're pinned to >=2.6.0,<2.12.0 in pyproject.toml. I want to upgrade to the latest version. The current producer is synchronous but since we're on FastAPI, it'd be great to switch to the async API if the new version supports it.
evals.expected_output: Updates pyproject.toml version pin, migrates producer from sync Producer to AIOProducer with AsyncAvroSerializer, updates imports

assertions.name: async_producer_import
assertions.description: producer.py now imports AIOProducer from confluent_kafka.aio

assertions.name: async_serializer_import
assertions.description: producer.py uses the async Avro serializer

assertions.name: async_migration_explained
assertions.description: Output explains the sync-to-async migration and why it fits FastAPI

evals.prompt: We upgraded our payment producer to confluent-kafka 2.13.0 last week and it's now throwing TypeErrors in production on the config values. We need this fixed ASAP — either roll back to 2.12.0 or fix the code, whatever gets us stable fastest. We're also on Python 3.7 which I know might be an issue. Here's the producer code and requirements.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the type hint enforcement issue in 2.13.0 AND the Python 3.7 incompatibility with 2.12.1+. Recommends downgrading to 2.12.0 (last version supporting Python 3.7 without type enforcement) OR fixing config types AND upgrading Python. Explains the tradeoffs.

assertions.name: identifies_type_error_cause
assertions.description: Explains that the TypeError is from 2.13.0's type hint enforcement on config values

assertions.name: identifies_python37_issue
assertions.description: Flags that Python 3.7 is incompatible with 2.12.1+ and factors this into the recommendation

assertions.name: offers_downgrade_option
assertions.description: Suggests downgrading to 2.12.0 as a viable quick fix given the Python 3.7 constraint

assertions.name: version_changed_in_requirements
assertions.description: requirements.txt version is changed — either downgraded to 2.12.0 or updated with a fix path

assertions.name: explains_tradeoffs
assertions.description: Discusses the tradeoff between downgrading (quick fix, stays on 3.7) vs fixing forward (fix types + upgrade Python)
